rows.title: Philippines Supreme Court A.M. No. 25-11-28-SC (AI Governance in Judiciary)
rows.summary: Proposed Governance Framework on the Use of Human-Centered Augmented Intelligence in the Judiciary. Risk-based classification (prohibited / high-risk / limited-risk / minimal-risk), prior authorization, risk assessment, auditability, monitoring, incident reporting. Rule that AI must not serve as the sole, primary, or determinative basis of any adjudicatory outcome. Issued En Banc 18 February 2026.

rows.title: India IT Amendment Rules on SGI
Germany AI Market Surveillance and Innovation Act (KI-MIG)
Uzbekistan Law No. ZRU-1115 on Artificial Intelligence
Philippines DepEd Order No. 003, s. 2026 (AI in Education)
rows.summary: DepEd issuance establishing risk-based classification of AI applications in basic education. Requires Privacy Impact Assessments, registration of AI systems in a centralized registry, mandatory safeguards and human oversight. Classifies certain AI applications (e.g., emotion recognition tied to decision-making) as posing unacceptable risks absent safeguards.

rows.title: Nigeria National Digital Economy and E-Governance Bill
India DPIIT Working Paper on Generative AI and Copyright
India AI (Ethics and Accountability) Bill 2025
rows.summary:
The Artificial Intelligence (Ethics and Accountability) Bill 2025, introduced in Lok Sabha, establishes a regulatory framework for AI technologies used in decision-making, surveillance, and algorithmic systems in India. It applies to AI developers and deployers across the country. The bill has not yet been enacted and requires Parliamentary approval before coming into force.

rows.title: Hong Kong Ethical AI Framework (Version 2.0, December 2025)
Nigeria NCC Internet Code of Practice 2025
Denmark Supplementary AI Provisions Act (Law No. 467/2025)
Argentina Bill 4243-D-2025 (Personal Data Protection in AI Systems)
Philippines Artificial Intelligence Regulation Act (SB 25)
rows.summary: Senate Bill 25. Pending in the 20th Congress. One of multiple proposals seeking to establish a comprehensive Philippine AI regulatory framework, including a national AI council/commission and tiered obligations for high-risk AI systems.

rows.title: Saudi Arabia Draft Global AI Hub Law
Mexico Federal AI Law (Ethical, Sovereign and Inclusive Development)
Virginia High-Risk AI Developer and Deployer Act (HB 2094)
Philippines BSP Exposure Draft — Amendments to IT Risk Management to Implement AFASA Section 6
rows.summary: Draft circular amending Section 148 MORB / 147-Q/145-S/142-P/126-N MORNBFI on IT Risk Management, implementing Section 6 of RA 12010 (AFASA). Defines blacklist screening, browser automation, and fortifies IT controls for electronic products and services. Under public consultation (2025).

rows.title: China Regulation on Network Data Security Management
Philippines NPC Advisory No. 2024-04 on AI Systems
rows.summary: NPC advisory addressing AI systems under the Data Privacy Act. Establishes that the deployer, as personal information controller (PIC), bears the primary "accountability" burden — including for biased or unlawful automated decisions, even where the underlying flaw was created by a third-party developer.

rows.title: Philippines COMELEC Resolution No. 11064-A (Amendment to AI Election Guidelines)
rows.summary: Amendment to COMELEC Resolution No. 11064. Promulgated 13 November 2024. Refines obligations on candidates and political parties for AI-generated content disclosure and platform-account management. No longer in force after 2025 election cycle (per AI Note).

rows.title: Philippines COMELEC Resolution No. 11064 (AI in Election Campaigns)
rows.summary: Guidelines on the Use of Social Media, Artificial Intelligence, and Internet Technology for Digital Election Campaign and the Prohibition and Punishment of its Misuse for Disinformation and Misinformation in connection with the 2025 National and Local Elections and BARMM Parliamentary Elections. Promulgated 17 September 2024. No longer in force after 2025 election cycle (per AI Note).

rows.title: Oman National Programme for AI and Advanced Digital Technologies
Council of Europe Framework Convention on AI
China Measures for Labeling of AI-Generated Synthetic Content
California Transparency in Frontier AI Act
Philippines BSP Memorandum M-2024-029 (AFASA IT Risk Management)
rows.summary: Reiterates the Guidelines on Risk Management Systems and Controls to Protect Financial Accounts in relation to Section 6 of Republic Act No. 12010 (Anti-Financial Account Scamming Act / AFASA). Effective from AFASA effectivity date (13 August 2024). Requires MFA, fraud-management systems, and account-owner enrolment/verification proportionate to operations.

rows.title: Philippines National AI Strategy Roadmap 2.0 (NAISR 2.0)
rows.summary: National AI Strategy Roadmap 2.0. Issued by the Department of Trade and Industry in July 2024; comprehensive blueprint to position the Philippines as a competitive AI economy. Policy framework, not a binding instrument.

rows.title: Philippines Deepfake Accountability and Transparency Act (HB 10567)
rows.summary: House Bill 10567 — Deepfake Accountability and Transparency Act. Pending. Targets accountability and disclosure obligations for AI-generated synthetic media.

rows.title: UAE Charter for the Development and Use of Artificial Intelligence
China Security Management Measures for Facial Recognition Technology
Japan AI Guidelines for Business v1.1
Philippines NPC Fees Advisory (March 2024)
rows.summary: NPC operational advisory dated 15 March 2024 announcing migration of online payments for fees and charges to LANDBANK Link.Biz.Portal effective 25 March 2024. Provides per-service payment workflow for complaints, advisory opinions, mediation, registration, and other NPC services.

rows.title: Vietnam Law on Digital Technology Industry
Saudi Arabia SDAIA Generative AI Guidelines
Peru Law No. 32082 (AI Interoperability in Consular Offices)
Philippines Internet Transactions Act of 2023 (RA 11967)
rows.summary: Republic Act No. 11967. Regulates e-commerce activities — digital platforms, e-marketplaces, e-retailers, online merchants — including AI-enabled systems used in covered transactions. Imposes consumer-protection and platform obligations and requires compliance with applicable data-privacy and information-security standards.

rows.title: China Scientific and Technological Ethics Review Regulation
Philippines NPC Circular 2023-02 (Data Privacy Competency Program)
rows.summary: NPC Circular 2023-02 dated 26 September 2023 establishing the Data Privacy Competency Program (DPCP). Operationalises NPC PHIL-DPO Program including DPO ACE training and Training-the-Trainers (T3) Program to capacitate Data Protection Officers in DPA compliance.

rows.title: Philippines BSP Memorandum M-2023-013 (Transaction Monitoring System Guidance Paper)
rows.summary: Guidance Paper for an Effective AML/CTPF Transaction Monitoring System. Approved by Monetary Board Resolution No. 490 (13 April 2023). Sets supervisory expectations for ML-driven transaction monitoring, alerts/case management, and suspicious-transaction investigation across BSFIs.

rows.title: India Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023
Philippines NPC Circular 2022-04 (ADM & profiling registration)
rows.summary: Imposes registration of data processing systems with mandatory notification where automated decision-making or profiling is the sole basis of a decision that significantly affects a data subject. Requires disclosure of methods and logic used, lawful basis, retention period, and the possible decisions affecting the data subject’s rights and freedoms.

rows.title: Philippines BSP Circular No. 1153 (Regulatory Sandbox Framework)
rows.summary: Institutionalizes the BSP Regulatory Sandbox Framework (Section 115 MORB / 1/2-Q/115-S/115-P/115-T/116-NA/41-CC MORNBFI). Test-and-learn approach for transformative financial technologies including AI/ML applications. Approved by Monetary Board Resolution No. 1217 dated 18 August 2022.

rows.title: Egypt Personal Data Protection Law (Law No. 151/2020)
Philippines Cybercrime Prevention Act of 2012 (RA 10175)
rows.summary: Republic Act No. 10175. Applies to AI-enabled conduct involving cybercrimes — computer-related fraud, identity theft, cyber libel, illegal access, data interference. Provides legal basis for investigation, electronic evidence collection, prosecution. Section 21 grants extraterritorial jurisdiction where any element of the offence occurs in the Philippines, where a computer system is local, or wher

rows.title: Philippines Data Privacy Act of 2012 (RA 10173)
rows.summary: Republic Act No. 10173 — the core framework governing personal data processing in the Philippines, including AI-enabled processing. Section 6 provides extraterritorial reach: applies where processing relates to personal information of Philippine citizens or residents, or where the entity has a local nexus (presence, business, or data collection in the country).

rows.title: China Administrative Measures on Internet-based Information Services
Regulation 2018/1725
rows.summary: Purpose: It replaces Regulation (EC) 45/2001 to align with the stricter principles of the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

rows.title: Philippines Public Advisory on Online Lending Platforms (DICT-NPC-SEC)
rows.summary: DICT-NPC-SEC joint advisory on Online Lending Platforms (OLPs). Reiterates prohibitions against unnecessary processing of personal data through mobile applications, unauthorized access to borrowers' contact lists, harassment, intimidation, public shaming, and debt-collection practices outside guarantors. Addresses lending companies, financing companies, and OLP operators.

rows.title: Philippines Konektadong Pinoy Act IRR
rows.summary: Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Konektadong Pinoy Act. Imposes cybersecurity-related obligations relevant to AI systems — impact assessments, audits, certification mechanisms, and reporting of material incidents to DICT (including coordination with national CERT structures via NCERT).

rows.title: Philippines E-Governance Act of 2022 (RA 11932)
rows.summary: Governs AI-enabled systems insofar as they fall within "emerging technologies" deployed as part of E-Government programmes. Requires compliance with the E-Government Master Plan (EGMP) and Philippine Government Interoperability Framework (PGIF), and applicable data privacy, cybersecurity and information-security standards. DICT issues technical standards and ensures interoperability across governm

rows.title: Philippines NPC Advisory No. 2026-01 (Guidelines on Data Scraping of Publicly Available Personal Data)
rows.summary: NPC Advisory No. 2026-01 dated 13 April 2026. Issues guidelines on the data scraping of publicly available personal data — addressing whether and how scraping of publicly accessible personal information falls within the Data Privacy Act's scope, and the conditions under which Personal Information Controllers and Processors may lawfully scrape such data.
